---
title: "Animated plot sequences"
subtitle: "Authoring reference — not published"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

Animations are produced by the build, not by content. You write **a function that returns a
sequence of plots** in a sidecar module; the pre-render hook
[`scripts/build_animations.py`](../../../scripts/build_animations.py) combines the frames into an
artifact while the site renders, and your page embeds that artifact. The notebook/slide imports
nothing — so it also stays Colab-safe.

## 1. Write the sidecar

Create `content/<collection>/<item>/animations/<name>.py`. It imports only numpy/matplotlib —
never the build:

```python
# content/blog-posts/my-post/animations/wave.py
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

FPS = 10
OUTPUTS = ("player",)          # any of: "player", "gif", "mp4"  -- the selection knob

def frames():                   # a generator keeps one figure open at a time
    x = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 200)
    for i in range(20):
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3))
        ax.plot(x, np.sin(x - 0.3 * i))
        ax.set_ylim(-1.1, 1.1)
        ax.set_title(f"frame {i}")
        yield fig
```

Every frame must rasterise to the same pixel size (consistent `figsize`/`dpi`). Instead of
`frames()` you may define `make_figure(i)` plus a module-level `FRAMES` count.

## 2. Choose the output(s) with `OUTPUTS`

The same function renders different artifacts depending on how it's declared:

| `OUTPUTS` | Artifact under `_generated/animations/<collection>/<item>/` | Use it for |
|---|---|---|
| `("player",)` | `<name>.html` — interactive player | Embed in a post/slide; step Prev/Next or press play |
| `("gif",)` | `<name>.gif` | Drop on another page as a tool, or external slides |
| `("mp4",)` | `<name>.mp4` (needs system ffmpeg) | A real video file |
| `("player", "gif")` | both | In-page player **and** a downloadable file |

## 3. Embed the artifact

The page references the generated **file** (relative path from the rendered page to
`_generated/`), never the helper.

Interactive player (post or RevealJS slide):

```html
<iframe src="../../../_generated/animations/blog-posts/my-post/wave.html"
        width="100%" height="430" style="border:0"
        onload="this.style.height=this.contentWindow.document.documentElement.scrollHeight+'px'"></iframe>
```

The `onload` sizes the iframe to the player's content height (same-origin) so the control bar is
never clipped; the player image scales to the iframe width.

Flat gif as a tool on some other page:

```markdown
![Wave](../../../_generated/animations/blog-posts/my-post/wave.gif)
```

## Preview while authoring

`_generated/` is rebuilt on every render. To build just the animations without a full site render:

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_animations.py            # build all sidecars
./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_animations.py --discover-only   # list them
```

A live example ships in the privacy-auditing post:
[`content/blog-posts/privacy-auditing/animations/threshold-sweep.py`](../../../content/blog-posts/privacy-auditing/animations/threshold-sweep.py).
